full_text_rationale: Full text describes AdaExplore building cross-task memory of validity rules from execution failures for Triton/kernel generation and evaluating runtime optimization on KernelBench.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 007; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Weihua Du, Jingming Zhuo, Yixin Dong, Andre He, Weiwei Sun, Zeyu Zheng, Manupa Karunaratne, Ivan Fox, Tim Dettmers, Tianqi Chen, Yiming Yang, Sean Welleck
titulo: AdaExplore: Failure-Driven Adaptation and Diversity-Preserving Search for Efficient Kernel Generation
formato_armazenamento: Cross-task skill memory of validity rules distilled from recurring execution failures on synthesized Triton/kernel tasks, plus local working memory from recent search-path states and a representative kernel pool as progress storage.
framework_agente: AdaExplore failure-driven adaptation and diversity-preserving kernel search agent
llm_utilizado: GPT-5-mini as default multi-pass model; GPT-5, Claude-4.6-Opus, Qwen3-Coder-Next in comparisons
benchmarks_utilizados: KernelBench Level-2 and Level-3, FlashInfer-Bench, TritonBench-T transfer checks
metricas_desempenho: Accuracy, speedup over PyTorch eager baseline, Fast@1.2, Fast@2, pass rates with and without skill memory, ablation speedup
baseline_comparado: Single-pass GPT-5-mini/GPT-5/Claude-4.6-Opus, AutoTriton, DR. Kernel, parallel sampling, iterative refinement, OpenEvolve, variants with cross-task skill memory, and AdaExplore ablations
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: From 200 synthesized tasks, AdaExplore collects 1,178 raw experiences, deduplicates to 174 unique experiences, and retains 78 high-frequency memory items.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; inference budgets are reported in steps rather than monetary/token cost.
resultado_principal: AdaExplore reaches 100% accuracy and 3.12x speedup on KernelBench Level-2 at 100 steps, and 100% accuracy and 1.72x speedup on Level-3. At 50 steps it reaches 2.65x Level-2 speedup and 1.55x Level-3 speedup.
desempenho_vs_baseline: At 50 steps on Level-2, AdaExplore reaches 2.65x speedup versus IR with skill memory at 2.59x and PS with skill memory at 2.12x. Removing cross-task skill memory drops speedup from 2.65x to 2.32x and Fast@1.2 from 71% to 56%.
custo_vs_baseline: NR; increased compute from 50 to 100 and 200 steps improves speedup, but no monetary or token-cost baseline is extracted.
limitacoes: Kernel-generation focus in Triton and GPU-runtime optimization; skill memory can bias some search strategies toward conservative updates; no cost/token reporting captured.
ameacas_validade: KernelBench task mix, GPU hardware, timing methodology, synthetic task generation, memory filtering thresholds, and model/backbone choices may affect generalization.
